evals.prompt: I'm building a payment processing service in my Laravel app. Write a ProcessPayment class with a handle() method that processes a payment for a given order ID, ensuring only one payment attempt can run at a time per order. Use the best available Laravel API for atomic locking.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes the search through a subagent, then writes the implementation based on results
expectations: The agent describes using a subagent or isolated subprocess to run the documentation search
The agent describes what query the subagent would run, using technical terms not natural language
The solution uses a Cache facade method or class for atomic locking
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the subagent returned from the documentation

evals.prompt: My Laravel app has an Order model with customer and items relationships. Iterating orders and accessing order->customer causes N+1 queries. Show me how to fix this globally so ALL model relationship access is automatically optimized, without manually adding with() to every query.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes the search through a subagent, then writes the implementation based on results
expectations: The agent describes using a subagent or isolated subprocess to run the documentation search
The agent describes what query the subagent would run, using technical terms not natural language
The solution addresses the N+1 problem globally, not just by suggesting with() on a single query
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the subagent returned from the documentation

evals.prompt: I need to look up the Laravel docs for three things: how to paginate queries, how to use soft deletes, and how to scope queries. Retrieve all three efficiently.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses a subagent to batch multiple searches in one call, returning only relevant excerpts
expectations: The agent describes using a subagent or isolated subprocess to run the documentation searches
The agent batches all three topics into a single subagent call rather than launching three separate searches
The subagent returns a summary or relevant excerpt, not the full raw documentation dump
The main agent response is concise — it works with the subagent's distilled output, not the raw docs

evals.prompt: Search the Laravel docs for how to configure mail drivers, then use that to write a MailService class that sends a welcome email.
evals.expected_output: Agent isolates the search in a subagent; main context only receives a summary, not the full docs
expectations: The agent describes routing the documentation search through a subagent
The agent describes what the subagent returns as a concise summary or relevant snippet
The main agent does NOT reproduce the full documentation in its response
The implementation is based on what the subagent returned, not on the agent's training-data knowledge alone
